_observed: Common eBay junk pattern — damaged Base Set Charizard listed at a low price that passes outlier rejection (because credible Base Set Charizard prices in 'Damaged' grade do legitimately reach $15-25). Title-quality filter passes (no bulk/lot/proxy keywords). The condition keyword 'damaged' (also 'creased') is what catches this at Stage 3. Without the condition gate, a buyer expecting a card that's a 'deal' clicks through to a creased, sleeve-damaged listing.
title: Charizard Base Set Holo #4 - Damaged, creased corners, water damage spots
